Xii . the punishment of perjury . and so much for those reason which be take from the greatness of this sin . the second sort may be draw from the grievousness of their punishment ; the which the lord inflict either mediate by the ministry of man , or immediate by himself . among man as it have in all age , § . Sect. 1. 1. perjury be punish with shameful infamy . and in all place be account a heinous sin and capital crime ; so have it be punish with no light or small punishment . for first , whereas a good name be better than either silver , gold , or great riches , Prou. 22.1 . and as high value among virtuous man as life it self ; humana , dedecus : divine punishment inflict upon perjure person for their false swear be destruction ; humane , be shame and discredit . and this be that punishment which both the civil and canon law impose upon such ; CHAP. vi . the manifold and great evil which acccmpanie unjust anger . and so much for the kind of anger . § . Sect. 1. now we be to set down the preservative , whereby we may be preserve from this sickness of the soul , and some medicine to cure we when we be fall into it . but for as much as it be in vain to prescribe physic , unless the patient will take it ; and see few or none be willing to take physic unless they see the necessity thereof , in respect of the dangerousness of their disease ; i will first show the greatness and malignity of this sickness of the mind , unjust anger , and afterward prescribe the remedy . the greatness and dangerousness of this disease appear by those great evil which it work , the dangerousness of anger show by the evil which it work . and that both private and public . the private evil concern either our self or our neighbour . the evil which concern our self be incident to the whole man , or to his several part , the body and the soul . the evil which anger bring to the whole man be diverse . first , the evil which anger bring to the whole man . 1. unjust anger deface god image . it overthrow peitie . Ioh. 4.20 . it deface in we the image of God ; for whereas the image of God do special consist in the virtue and grace of the mind , anger overthrow they all , and first of all piety which be the chief of all , and the sum of the first table . it extinguish the love of God : for how shall we love God who we have not see , if we do not love our neighbour who we have see ? 1. Ioh. 4.20 . and how do we love our neighbour , if upon no cause or every trifle cause , we be incense to injust anger against he ? it overthrow the principal part of God worship , invocation : for if we will compass the altar , and there offer up the sacrifice of prayer and thanksgiving , we must first wash our hand in innocence : Psal. 26.6 . 1.
